message:
wa-ogc.2: environment detection for tutorial exercises

Add TutorialEnvironment that adapts exercises to detected runtime state
(WezTerm, agents, watcher, DB, config). Each exercise declares Requirements
and can_simulate flag; can_run_exercise() returns Yes/Simulation/No.
13 tests cover all Requirement variants and CanRun paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","title":"[Human command] `wa doctor` (environment + config + DB checks)","description":"# Task: Human command wa doctor

## Goal
Provide a deterministic checklist that validates the wa environment.

Doctor is a user trust surface: it must make failures actionable and help users understand where wa stores state.

## Checks (initial)
- Workspace/config resolution
  - print resolved workspace root
  - print resolved DB/log/lock/socket paths
  - verify workspace is writable

- WezTerm
  - wezterm CLI available (wezterm cli list works)
  - wezterm version meets minimum supported
  - scrollback settings are sufficient (warn if too low; best-effort)

- Storage
  - DB is readable/writable
  - schema/migration version matches
  - WAL + foreign_keys configured

- Watcher status (if running)
  - last tick time
  - tailer count
  - backlog sizes

- Feature availability
  - browser (playwright)
  - mcp
  - web/tui/sync (optional)

## Output
- Rich panels when TTY.
- Non-TTY JSON suitable for automation.

## Safety
- Output must never include secrets (even if config contains tokens).
- Redaction must be applied to any potentially sensitive field.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.8` (fixture-based checks + output stability)
- E2E: `wa-4vx.10.22` (healthy/broken scenarios with verbose artifacts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a healthy system, wa doctor exits 0 with a concise summary.
- On a broken system, it exits non-zero and lists actionable fixes.
- Output never includes secrets (even if config contains tokens).

Added --json flag that outputs structured JSON with all diagnostic checks, runtime health, crash history, and circuit breakers. JSON includes: ok (bool), status (ok/warning/error), version, checks array with name/status/detail/recommendation per check, optional circuits array. Plain text output preserved unchanged. Also fixed pre-existing test issue (verbose: false → verbose: 0 type mismatch). All tests pass: 20 wa tests, 1139+101 wa-core tests.","created_at":"2026-01-29T06:42:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","title":"Expose watcher health snapshot in CLI (status/health command)","description":"# Task: Expose watcher health snapshot

## Goal
Make the watcher’s health state visible so we can quickly diagnose:
- stuck tailers
- slow polling
- DB backpressure
- pattern match overload

## Metrics (example)
- discovery tick duration
- tailer count + per-domain breakdown
- per-pane last_seen + last_seq
- storage write queue depth
- event bus queue depths
- FTS insert latency

## Deliverables
- `wa status --health` (or `wa health`) prints the snapshot.
- Robot/MCP can access the same snapshot if useful.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - health snapshot JSON is schema-parseable and stable
  - “stuck pane” synthetic scenario is surfaced clearly

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (stable JSON output in non-TTY mode)

- Diagnostic coverage:
  - `wa doctor` tests (`wa-nu4.3.4.8`) should validate that health signals are consistent with watcher state when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a pane stops producing output, health snapshot highlights it clearly.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.3","title":"Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized)

## Goal
Generate a single artifact users can attach to bug reports that contains:
- environment info (OS, wa version)
- config summary (redacted)
- DB health stats (counts, schema version, WAL size)
- last N events + workflow step logs (redacted)
- **active pane reservations + recent reservation conflicts (redacted)**

## Safety requirements
- Never include secrets.
- Apply PolicyEngine redaction rules to any text fields.
- Prefer summaries over raw transcripts.

## Output
- A directory or archive (e.g. `.tar.zst`) written to a user-specified path.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests: `wa-nu4.3.4.7` validates:
  - redaction (no secrets)
  - stable file layout
  - stable metadata fields
  - works while watcher is running

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle can be generated while watcher is running.
- Bundle contents are stable and documented.


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.9.2: Implement config loader: defaults + file + env + CLI overrides; show effective config ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.6: Retention & hygiene: prune old segments, maintenance log, explicit vacuum command ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.7: Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets) ● P2

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.4","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.5","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.
","notes":"Review: repo already includes metrics endpoint implementation and CLI flags (files: crates/wa-core/src/metrics.rs, crates/wa/src/main.rs, metrics config).  {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.6","title":"[Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export)","description":"# Task: `wa diag bundle`

## Goal
Provide the CLI surface described in the plan:
- `wa diag bundle --last 15m --output <path>`

This should wrap the diagnostic bundle generator (`wa-nu4.3.4.3`) and ensure the UX is great:
- clear progress logging
- clear output location
- stable exit codes

## Requirements
- Redaction always on by default.
- If requested output path exists, refuse unless `--force`.
- Include enabled features + versions in the bundle.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.4.7` (bundle tests) and should include:
  - CLI exit codes and refusal behavior (`--force`)
  - stable metadata fields
  - no secrets in stdout/stderr or bundle contents

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `wa diag bundle` produces a bundle containing:
  - config summary (redacted)
  - versions
  - recent events + workflow logs
  - health snapshot
  - audit excerpts (if enabled)

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.7","title":"Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets)","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle tests

## Goal
Ensure diagnostic bundles are reliable and safe.

## Testing
- Bundle layout contains required files.
- Redaction applied (no secrets in outputs).
- Stable metadata present (`version`, `enabled_features`).
- Reservation snapshot present when reservations exist (owner/TTL, redacted).
- If bundle already exists and `--force` not set, command fails safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a running WezTerm instance (use fixture DB and fixture config).


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-ogc.1","title":"Tutorial engine scaffolding: state machine, progress persistence, command wiring","description":"# Tutorial engine scaffolding

## Purpose
Build the core infrastructure for the interactive tutorial system: a state machine that tracks progress, persists state, and integrates with the wa CLI.

## Background
The tutorial needs to:
1. Track which exercises have been completed
2. Persist progress across sessions
3. Provide a clean CLI interface (`wa learn`, `wa learn <track>`, etc.)

## Implementation Details

### State Machine
```rust
pub struct TutorialState {
    current_track: Option<TrackId>,
    current_exercise: Option<ExerciseId>,
    completed_exercises: HashSet<ExerciseId>,
    achievements: Vec<Achievement>,
    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    last_active: DateTime<Utc>,
}

pub enum TutorialEvent {
    StartTrack(TrackId),
    CompleteExercise(ExerciseId),
    SkipExercise(ExerciseId),
    UnlockAchievement(Achievement),
    Reset,
}
```

### Progress File
Location: `~/.config/wa/learn.json`
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "completed_exercises": ["basics.1", "basics.2"],
  "achievements": ["first_watch", "first_event"],
  "last_track": "basics",
  "started_at": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  "total_time_minutes": 15
}
```

### CLI Integration
- `wa learn` - Resume or show track selection
- `wa learn basics` - Start/resume Track 1
- `wa learn --reset` - Clear progress
- `wa learn --status` - Show completion summary

## Testing
- Unit tests for state machine transitions
- Integration tests for file persistence
- Golden tests for CLI output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TutorialState struct with FromJson/ToJson
- [ ] Progress file created/updated correctly
- [ ] CLI subcommands wired and functional
- [ ] State machine handles all transitions
- [ ] Tests pass (state machine, persistence, CLI)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:45:44.707983594Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T05:04:48.599163982Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T05:04:48.599087277Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.1","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ogc.10","title":"Tutorial tests: state machine, progress, golden output, E2E scenarios","description":"# Tutorial tests (core tracks)

## Purpose
Comprehensive test coverage for the tutorial system’s **core** experience (engine + Tracks 1–4) to ensure reliability and prevent regressions.

Track 5 (Advanced / coordination) is intentionally tracked separately so it can depend on Phase 5 features without causing priority inversions.

## Test Categories

### 1. State Machine Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn tutorial_state_transitions() {
    let mut state = TutorialState::new();
    state.apply(TutorialEvent::StartTrack(TrackId::Basics));
    assert_eq!(state.current_track, Some(TrackId::Basics));

    state.apply(TutorialEvent::CompleteExercise("basics.1".into()));
    assert!(state.completed_exercises.contains(&"basics.1".into()));
}

#[test]
fn tutorial_rejects_invalid_transitions() {
    let state = TutorialState::new();
    // Can't complete exercise without starting track
    assert!(state.can_apply(TutorialEvent::CompleteExercise("basics.1".into())).is_err());
}
```

### 2. Progress Persistence Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn progress_roundtrips() {
    let state = TutorialState::with_completions(vec!["basics.1", "basics.2"]);
    let json = serde_json::to_string(&state).unwrap();
    let loaded: TutorialState = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(state.completed_exercises, loaded.completed_exercises);
}

#[test]
fn progress_file_created_on_first_exercise() {
    let tmp = tempdir().unwrap();
    let ctx = TutorialContext::new(tmp.path());
    ctx.complete_exercise("basics.1");
    assert!(tmp.path().join("learn.json").exists());
}
```

### 3. Golden Output Tests (Tracks 1–4)
For each exercise in Tracks 1–4, snapshot test the output:
```rust
#[test]
fn track1_exercise1_output() {
    let output = render_exercise(Track::Basics, 1);
    insta::assert_snapshot!(output);
}
```

### 4. Environment Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn detects_wezterm_running() {
    let env = TutorialEnvironment::detect_with_mock(MockWezterm::Running(3));
    assert!(env.wezterm_running);
    assert_eq!(env.pane_count, 3);
}
```

### 5. Sandbox Mode Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn sandbox_intercepts_send() {
    let mut sandbox = SandboxContext::new();
    let result = sandbox.execute(Command::WaSend { pane: 0, text: "test" });
    assert!(result.is_simulated());
    assert!(sandbox.sends.contains(&(0, "test")));
}
```

### 6. E2E Scenario Tests (core)
```bash
# Full track completion in sandbox mode
./scripts/e2e_tutorial.sh --track basics --sandbox
# Verify achievements unlocked
./scripts/e2e_tutorial.sh --verify-achievements first_watch
```

## Coverage Requirements
- State machine: 100% branch coverage
- Progress persistence: all edge cases (missing file, corrupt file)
- Golden output: all exercises for Tracks 1–4
- Environment detection: all conditions
- Sandbox: all intercepted commands

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] State machine tests pass
- [ ] Persistence tests pass
- [ ] Golden output snapshots for Tracks 1–4
- [ ] Environment detection tests pass
- [ ] Sandbox mode tests pass
- [ ] E2E core tutorial scenario passes in CI
- [ ] Coverage meets requirements


## Testing
- “Tests for tests”:
  - Add at least one intentionally-failing snapshot (behind a flag or CI-only mode) to prove:
    - snapshots are actually executed
    - failure output points to the exact exercise

## E2E logging requirements
- Tutorial E2E scenarios must log:
  - track/exercise IDs
  - transitions taken
  - completion/achievement outputs
- Artifacts must include the progress file and a final summary snapshot.

 {"id":"wa-ogc.11","title":"Track 5 tests: Advanced tutorial scenarios + snapshots","description":"# Tutorial tests (Track 5: Advanced)

## Purpose
Add the dedicated tests + E2E scenarios for **Track 5 (Advanced)**, which depends on Phase 5 features (custom patterns + multi-agent coordination).

This is separated from core tutorial tests so we can keep core onboarding high-priority while allowing Track 5 to depend on later systems.

## Scope
- Golden output snapshots for Track 5 exercises.
- Integration tests that validate Track 5’s environment detection/gating behaves correctly:
  - if coordination features are not enabled/available, Track 5 should be skipped with an actionable message.

## E2E scenarios
- Run Track 5 in sandbox/simulated mode first.
- If Phase 5 coordination is available in the test rig, run a “realistic” scenario that:
  - creates a small multi-pane setup
  - demonstrates safe broadcast/coordination primitives
  - verifies audit + policy gates

## Logging & artifacts
- Must follow the standard E2E harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`):
  - per-step timestamps + durations
  - artifacts include stdout/stderr, relevant exports, and redaction checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- Track 5 golden outputs are snapshot-tested.
- Track 5 E2E scenarios run (or skip deterministically with explanation) and produce debuggable artifacts.


## Testing
- Ensure Track 5 tests are hermetic:
  - no real credentials
  - no real destructive actions
  - rely on simulation/sandbox where possible

-{"id":"wa-ogc.2","title":"Environment detection: detect WezTerm, agents, config state for contextual exercises","description":"# Environment detection for tutorial

## Purpose
Detect the user's environment to provide contextual exercises and skip irrelevant steps. A user without WezTerm running shouldn't do "start watching" exercises.

## Background
Different users have different setups:
- WezTerm running vs not
- Agents present vs empty panes
- wa configured vs fresh install
- Existing DB vs first time

The tutorial should adapt to each situation.

## Implementation Details

### Environment Checks
```rust
pub struct TutorialEnvironment {
    wezterm_running: bool,
    wezterm_version: Option<String>,
    pane_count: usize,
    agent_panes: Vec<AgentInfo>, // Panes with detected agents
    wa_configured: bool,         // wa.toml exists
    db_has_data: bool,           // Segments/events exist
    shell_integration: bool,     // OSC 133 markers detected
}

impl TutorialEnvironment {
    pub async fn detect() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn can_run_exercise(&self, exercise: &Exercise) -> CanRun {
        match exercise.requirements {
            Requires::WeztermRunning if !self.wezterm_running => 
                CanRun::No("Start WezTerm first"),
            Requires::AgentPresent if self.agent_panes.is_empty() => 
                CanRun::Simulation("No agents detected, using simulation"),
            _ => CanRun::Yes,
        }
    }
}
```

### Exercise Requirements
Each exercise declares its requirements:
```rust
pub struct Exercise {
    id: ExerciseId,
    title: String,
    requirements: Vec<Requirement>,
    can_simulate: bool,  // True if exercise works in sandbox mode
}
```

### Adaptive Flow
1. User starts track
2. Detect environment
3. For each exercise:
   - Check requirements
   - If met: run exercise
   - If not met but can_simulate: run in sandbox
   - If not met and can't simulate: skip with explanation

## Testing
- Unit tests for each detection check
- Mock tests for different environment states
- Integration tests with real WezTerm (optional, CI skip)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TutorialEnvironment struct with all detection fields
- [ ] detect() function checks all conditions
- [ ] can_run_exercise() returns appropriate guidance
- [ ] Clear user messaging for skipped exercises
- [ ] Tests for all detection paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:45:59.975507421Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T17:45:59.975507421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.2","depends_on_id":"wa-dug.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.2","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.2","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"wa-ogc.2","title":"Environment detection: detect WezTerm, agents, config state for contextual exercises","description":"# Environment detection for tutorial

## Purpose

## Background

- Agents present vs empty panes
- wa configured vs fresh install
- Existing DB vs first time

The tutorial should adapt to each situation.

## Implementation Details

### Environment Checks
```rust
pub struct TutorialEnvironment {
    wezterm_running: bool,
    wezterm_version: Option<String>,
    pane_count: usize,
    agent_panes: Vec<AgentInfo>, // Panes with detected agents
    wa_configured: bool,         // wa.toml exists
    db_has_data: bool,           // Segments/events exist
    shell_integration: bool,     // OSC 133 markers detected
}

impl TutorialEnvironment {
    pub async fn detect() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn can_run_exercise(&self, exercise: &Exercise) -> CanRun {
        match exercise.requirements {
            Requires::WeztermRunning if !self.wezterm_running => 
                CanRun::No("Start WezTerm first"),
            Requires::AgentPresent if self.agent_panes.is_empty() => 
                CanRun::Simulation("No agents detected, using simulation"),
            _ => CanRun::Yes,
        }
    }
}
```

```rust
pub struct Exercise {

    requirements: Vec<Requirement>,
    can_simulate: bool,  // True if exercise works in sandbox mode
}
```

### Adaptive Flow

2. Detect environment
3. For each exercise:

 {"id":"wa-ogc.3","title":"Track 1: Basics - What is wa? Start watching. View status (5 min)","description":"# Track 1: Basics

## Purpose
Introduce wa to new users in under 5 minutes. They should understand what wa does, successfully start it, and see meaningful output.

## Target Audience
Users who just installed wa and want to understand what it does.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 1.1: What is wa?
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Brief explanation with ASCII diagram
- **Duration**: 30 seconds
- **Goal**: User understands wa is a "terminal hypervisor for AI agents"

### Exercise 1.2: Check WezTerm
- **Type**: Verification
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa doctor` (or tutorial auto-checks)
- **Success**: "✓ WezTerm detected with 2 panes"
- **Fallback**: Simulation mode shows mock output

### Exercise 1.3: Start Watching
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa watch` (tutorial may auto-start)
- **Success**: "wa is now watching 2 panes"
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.4: View Status
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa status`
- **Success**: Tutorial explains each column
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.5: Your First Event
- **Type**: Simulated (unless agent present)
- **Action**: Tutorial injects mock compaction event
- **Success**: User runs `wa events` and sees it
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement unlocked: "First Watch"
- Prompt to continue to Track 2

## Testing
- Golden output tests for each exercise explanation
- Integration test for full track in sandbox mode
- E2E test with real WezTerm (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5 exercises implemented
- [ ] Each exercise has clear instructions and success criteria
- [ ] Sandbox mode works for all exercises
- [ ] Track completion triggers achievement
- [ ] Total time <5 minutes in testing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:13.680730807Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:47:51.491116106Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-docs","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.3","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ogc.4","title":"Track 2: Events - Understanding detections and pattern matching (10 min)","description":"# Track 2: Events

## Purpose
Teach users how wa detects events using pattern matching. They'll understand the detection pipeline and learn to query events.

## Target Audience
Users who completed Track 1 and want to understand the "magic" behind detections.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 2.1: What are Events?
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Events are meaningful terminal occurrences (usage limits, compaction, errors)
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 2.2: Pattern Packs
- **Type**: Information + Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa rules list`
- **Content**: Explain built-in packs (core.codex, core.claude_code)
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 2.3: View Recent Events
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Requires**: Events in DB (or simulation)
- **Action**: User runs `wa events --limit 5`
- **Success**: Tutorial explains event fields (type, pane, time)
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 2.4: Search Events
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa query "compaction"`
- **Content**: Explain FTS search capabilities
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 2.5: Test a Pattern
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa rules test "Session limit reached"`
- **Success**: Shows which rule matches and why
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 2.6: Trigger a Detection
- **Type**: Simulated
- **Action**: Tutorial injects text matching core.codex.usage_limit
- **Success**: User sees new event appear in `wa events`
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement: "Pattern Detective"
- Prompt to continue to Track 3

## Testing
- Golden output tests for all exercise text
- Integration test with simulated events
- Pattern test exercise must work in sandbox

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 6 exercises implemented
- [ ] Pattern testing works in sandbox mode
- [ ] Event simulation creates visible detection
- [ ] Track completion triggers achievement
- [ ] Total time <10 minutes in testing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:26.802255100Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:26.802255100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-docs","area-patterns","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.4","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.4","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ogc.5","title":"Track 3: Workflows - Automating responses to events (15 min)","description":"# Track 3: Workflows

## Purpose
Teach users how wa can automatically respond to events. They'll understand the workflow system, policy gates, and safe automation.

## Target Audience
Users who understand events (Track 2) and want to automate responses.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 3.1: What are Workflows?
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Workflows = automated multi-step responses to events
- **Diagram**: Event → Workflow → Steps → Verification
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 3.2: Built-in Workflows
- **Type**: Information + Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa workflow list`
- **Content**: Explain handle_compaction, handle_usage_limits
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 3.3: Policy Gates
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Why wa asks permission before dangerous actions
- **Explain**: Allow/Deny/RequireApproval decisions
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 3.4: Run a Workflow Manually
- **Type**: Simulated Interactive
- **Action**: `wa workflow run handle_compaction --dry-run`
- **Success**: User sees step plan without execution
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Exercise 3.5: Workflow Step Logs
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User views workflow execution logs
- **Content**: Explain step states, timing, outcomes
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Exercise 3.6: Watch Workflow Execute
- **Type**: Simulated
- **Action**: Tutorial triggers event that fires workflow
- **Success**: User watches workflow complete steps
- **Duration**: 180 seconds

### Exercise 3.7: Approval Flow
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: Workflow hits RequireApproval gate
- **Success**: User runs `wa approve` and sees continuation
- **Duration**: 120 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement: "Workflow Wizard"
- Prompt to continue to Track 4

## Testing
- Simulated workflows must be visually convincing
- Approval flow must work in sandbox
- Step logs must show realistic data

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 7 exercises implemented
- [ ] Workflow list shows built-in workflows
- [ ] Dry-run mode displays step plan
- [ ] Simulated workflow execution is convincing
- [ ] Approval flow tutorial works in sandbox
- [ ] Track completion triggers achievement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:39.572597Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:39.572597Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-docs","area-ux","area-workflows","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.5","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-ogc.5","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}
